_doc.purpose: Approximate geographic footprint weights per ticker. Used by sector_overlays to weight regional time series (Case-Shiller per metro, state unemployment, regional retail) when computing the regional context for a name. Weights need not sum to 1.0 — they are relative; the overlay engine renormalizes.

schema.metros: metro_code -> weight (use the metro codes defined in data_catalog.registry, e.g. 'NYC', 'SF', 'LA', 'ATL', 'DAL', 'PHX', 'MIA', 'CHI', 'BOS', 'SEA', 'DCA', 'DEN', 'MIN', 'POR', 'LAS', 'DET')
schema.notes: Free-text explanation of how the weights were derived (10-K disclosure, store-count table, property list).

_doc.sourcing: Manually curated from each company's most recent 10-K (Properties section, Item 2) or annual store-count disclosure. Refresh when a major repositioning happens (e.g. WMT closes a region, PLD acquires a portfolio).
_doc.fallback: When a ticker is not in this file, the geography service falls back to LLM extraction against the cached 10-K text in filing_docs.

PLD.notes: Prologis logistics real estate concentrated in coastal infill markets — LA/Inland Empire is the single largest market by NOI.

AMT.notes: American Tower owns ~43k US sites distributed broadly; ~55% of revenue international. Domestic exposure tracks population density.

EQIX.notes: Equinix US data center footprint dominated by NoVA (Ashburn), Silicon Valley, NYC metro. Power-constrained markets command premium rents.

O.notes: Realty Income net-lease portfolio is highly diversified across 50 states; TX/FL/CA carry the highest dollar weights.

SPG.notes: Simon Property Group regional mall + premium outlet portfolio. FL/CA/TX outsized weight reflects population growth + tourism.

AVB.notes: AvalonBay multifamily portfolio is concentrated in supply-constrained coastal markets — Northeast / Mid-Atlantic + Pacific.

EQR.notes: Equity Residential portfolio overlaps AvalonBay's coastal footprint; modest Sun Belt expansion underway.

WELL.notes: Welltower senior housing + outpatient medical office; geography correlates with 75+ population density.

VICI.notes: VICI Properties largest landlord on Las Vegas Strip. Regional casino exposure across Mississippi River + Atlantic City.

IRM.notes: Iron Mountain records management + data centers. Geography mirrors enterprise / federal customer density.

WMT.notes: Walmart US store base ~4,600 stores. Sun Belt and Mid-South heavy by store count; CA underweight relative to population.

COST.notes: Costco US warehouse base ~600; California is HQ + the single biggest market by warehouse count + revenue.

HD.notes: Home Depot ~2,000 US stores. Sun Belt + South strongest by revenue; comp sales tightly correlated with existing home sales + housing-related credit.

LOW.notes: Lowe's ~1,700 US stores; HQ in NC. Strongest store density in South and Mid-Atlantic.

TGT.notes: Target ~1,950 US stores. Minneapolis HQ + dense Midwest + California presence; underexposed to deep South vs. Walmart.

KR.notes: Kroger banners across Midwest + South + Southwest. Underrepresented in Northeast vs. Albertsons.

DG.notes: Dollar General ~19,000 stores; concentrated in rural South and Mid-South — sensitive to low-income consumer credit metrics.

AMZN.notes: Amazon retail revenue mirrors US population + e-commerce penetration; fulfillment footprint Sun-Belt-heavy.

MCD.notes: McDonald's ~13,500 US stores. Comps sensitive to fuel prices (drive-thru traffic) + low/mid-income wage growth.

SBUX.notes: Starbucks ~16,400 US stores. Coastal + urban density much higher than QSR peers; price elasticity matters more.

CMG.notes: Chipotle ~3,400 US stores; concentrated in coastal urban + suburban Sun Belt.

JPM.notes: JPMorgan US consumer + commercial bank deposits broadest in NY, CA, TX, IL, OH. Wholesale banking less geographically tied.

BAC.notes: Bank of America retail deposits heaviest in NC (HQ), CA, FL. Strong East Coast + Sun Belt overlap with population growth.

WFC.notes: Wells Fargo legacy West Coast bank; deposit base CA-heavy with growing Texas + Southeast presence.

USB.notes: US Bancorp footprint Midwest + Pacific Northwest + Mountain West. Less exposed to coastal CRE than NYC banks.

PNC.notes: PNC Mid-Atlantic + Midwest stronghold; BBVA USA acquisition added Sun Belt presence (TX, AZ, AL, FL, NM, CO).

T.notes: AT&T wireless coverage broadly mirrors US population. Fiber footprint concentrated in legacy SBC/BellSouth states (TX/CA/Southeast).

VZ.notes: Verizon wireless ubiquitous; FiOS fiber footprint Northeast-heavy (legacy Bell Atlantic + NYNEX territory).

NEE.notes: NextEra = Florida Power & Light (regulated) + NextEra Energy Resources (national renewables). Regulated leg is ~100% Florida.

DUK.notes: Duke Energy regulated electric across Carolinas + Florida + Midwest (IN/OH/KY). Earnings tied to rate-base growth + cooling-degree days in SE.

SO.notes: Southern Company subsidiaries Georgia Power + Alabama Power + Mississippi Power. Earnings tied to Southeast load growth + Vogtle nuclear.

XOM.notes: ExxonMobil upstream concentrated Permian Basin (TX/NM) + Gulf of Mexico + Guyana (international). Downstream Baytown / Beaumont refining.

CVX.notes: Chevron upstream Permian + DJ Basin + Gulf of Mexico + international. California El Segundo / Richmond refining.

COP.notes: ConocoPhillips concentrated Permian + Eagle Ford + Bakken + Alaska Slope. No downstream so price exposure is pure E&P.
